cmd/jobs/quickstart.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for docker-compose quickstart helpers; its 'cron' command schedules billing-lifecycle polling loops via gocron. Explicitly NOT for production — billing-worker drives these flows via Kafka/Watermill instead.

patterns.name: gocron polling loops for billing lifecycle
patterns.description: cronjobs.go uses gocron.NewScheduler to schedule BillingSubscriptionReconciler.All (hourly), BillingCollector.All and BillingAutoAdvancer.All (per-minute) on internal.App; jobs run via gocron.DurationJob + gocron.NewTask passing cmd.Context().

patterns.name: Nil-guard optional internal.App services
patterns.description: ChargesAutoAdvancer may be nil when charges/credits are disabled; always nil-check before scheduling its job.
patterns.example: if internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer != nil { s.NewJob(gocron.DurationJob(time.Minute), gocron.NewTask(func() { internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer.All(cmd.Context(), namespaces) })) }

patterns.name: Block on context cancel then shutdown scheduler
patterns.description: Cron RunE calls s.Start(), blocks on <-cmd.Context().Done(), then s.Shutdown() for graceful stop.
patterns.example: <-cmd.Context().Done(); if err := s.Shutdown(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Meta-group composition via init()
patterns.description: quickstart.go aggregates sub-commands from other job packages (subscriptionsync) and cronjobs.go adds Cron, all through Cmd.AddCommand in init().

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'quickstart' parent command and composes sub-commands (subscriptionsync) via init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Documented as not for production — do not add production-critical logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Implements the 'cron' sub-command using gocron for billing-lifecycle automation in quickstart environments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcodes namespace 'default' and batchSize=10 (unsuitable for multi-tenant production). New cron jobs must nil-guard optional internal.App services. Job task errors are logged, not returned.

anti_patterns: Using these gocron patterns in production workers — billing-worker uses Kafka/Watermill
Not nil-checking optional internal.App services (e.g. ChargesAutoAdvancer) before scheduling
Adding production-critical billing logic to quickstart commands

decisions.decision: Quickstart cron uses gocron instead of Watermill/Kafka
decisions.rationale: The quickstart docker-compose environment may lack a full Kafka topology; gocron provides polling-based advancement without Kafka dependencies, keeping quickstart self-contained.

code_examples.scenario: Add a nil-guarded scheduled billing job

cmd/notification-service.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the notification-service: Wire assembles domain services, then main.go manually constructs the Watermill Kafka subscriber and notification consumer and runs them in an oklog/run.Group. Unlike other workers, subscriber + consumer.New live in main.go, not a common.Runner.

patterns.name: Manual run.Group assembly in main.go
patterns.description: main.go builds its own run.Group (telemetry server + notificationConsumer.Run + SignalHandler) rather than using common.Runner; group.Run uses run.WithReverseShutdownOrder().
patterns.example: group.Add(func() error { return notificationConsumer.Run(ctx) }, func(err error) { _ = notificationConsumer.Close() })

patterns.name: Subscriber constructed in main.go, not Wire
patterns.description: watermillkafka.NewSubscriber is called in main.go with app.BrokerOptions and conf.Notification.Consumer.ConsumerGroupName; it is not part of the Wire graph.

patterns.name: consumer.Options aggregates app fields
patterns.description: consumer.Options assembles SystemEventsTopic, Router (router.Options with subscriber/publisher/logger/meter/tracer), Marshaler, and Notification from app fields.
patterns.example: consumer.Options{SystemEventsTopic: conf.Events.SystemEvents.Topic, Router: router.Options{Subscriber: wmSubscriber, Publisher: app.MessagePublisher, ...}, Marshaler: app.EventPublisher.Marshaler(), Notification: app.Notification}

patterns.name: Application exposes raw primitives
patterns.description: The Application struct exposes BrokerOptions, MessagePublisher, metric.Meter, trace.Tracer, EventPublisher, and TelemetryServer directly so main.go can assemble consumer.Options manually.
patterns.example: type Application struct { ... BrokerOptions watermillkafka.BrokerOptions; MessagePublisher message.Publisher; Meter metric.Meter; Tracer trace.Tracer; TelemetryServer common.TelemetryServer }

key_file_guides.role: Parses config, Wire-inits app, Migrate, builds Kafka subscriber, constructs consumer.Options, instantiates consumer.New, runs the run.Group (telemetry server + consumer + signal handler).
key_file_guides.watch_for: consumer.Options.Marshaler must be app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() — a mismatched marshaler breaks message deserialization. ctx is context.Background() at the root (legitimate).

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written wire.Build (//go:build wireinject) listing provider sets; Application exposes more raw primitives than other workers for manual consumer construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.NotificationServiceProvisionTopics must be listed for correct Kafka topic provisioning. Uses common.Watermill (full publisher).

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire — DO NOT EDIT. TelemetryServer is an Application field (managed in this binary's run.Group), not on common.Runner.
key_file_guides.watch_for: StreamingConnector is wired as a transitive dependency even though notification-service does not query usage.

key_file_guides.role: ldflags-provisioned version plus vcs.revision/vcs.time read from debug.ReadBuildInfo in init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Defaults to 'unknown' when build info is unavailable.

anti_patterns: Moving Kafka subscriber construction into Wire — it stays in main.go so the consumer group name comes from config at startup
Using app.MessagePublisher as the marshaler source instead of app.EventPublisher.Marshaler()
Adding business notification logic to main.go — it belongs in openmeter/notification/consumer

decisions.decision: Watermill subscriber constructed in main.go rather than Wire
decisions.rationale: The consumer group name is a runtime config value tied to deployment identity; keeping subscriber construction in main.go makes the config-to-subscriber mapping explicit and avoids parameterising Wire with runtime strings.

code_examples.scenario: Assemble the notification consumer in main.go after Wire init

cmd/server.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the main HTTP API server: Wire-assembles all ~40 domain services, runs migrations, registers namespace handlers in strict order before initNamespace, provisions sandbox app and default billing profile, builds router.Config with every service, and runs an oklog/run.Group (API server, telemetry server, Kafka producer, notification event handler, termination checker).

patterns.name: Namespace handler registration before initNamespace
patterns.description: LedgerNamespaceHandler, KafkaIngestNamespaceHandler, and TaxCodeNamespaceHandler MUST be registered on app.NamespaceManager before initNamespace, or the default namespace is provisioned without them.

patterns.name: router.Config aggregates all domain services
patterns.description: server.NewServer receives a router.Config struct with every domain service as a named field. A new endpoint requires adding the service to router.Config (main.go) and to the Application struct in wire.go.
patterns.example: router.Config{ Billing: app.BillingRegistry.Billing, Customer: app.Customer, ChargeService: app.BillingRegistry.ChargesServiceOrNil(), ... }

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before server start
patterns.description: After Migrate: register namespace handlers -> initNamespace -> SandboxProvisioner -> ProvisionDefaultBillingProfile -> MeterConfigInitializer; each must succeed (os.Exit(1) on error) before server.NewServer and run.Group.

patterns.name: Five-component run.Group with graceful shutdown
patterns.description: main.go assembles a run.Group: telemetry server, Kafka producer group, API HTTP server, notification event handler, termination checker, plus signal handler — each with a paired interrupt/shutdown func.

patterns.name: ChargesServiceOrNil for credits-gated charge service
patterns.description: router.Config.ChargeService uses app.BillingRegistry.ChargesServiceOrNil() so it is nil-safe when credits.enabled=false.

key_file_guides.role: Orchestrates startup: config parse -> Wire init -> SetGlobals -> Migrate -> namespace handler registration -> initNamespace -> provisioning -> server.NewServer (with full router.Config) -> run.Group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new namespace.Handler must register BEFORE initNamespace(); any new post-migration provisioning step before run.Group.Run(). Errors during startup os.Exit(1).

key_file_guides.role: Largest hand-written wire.Build listing ~30 provider sets (common.App, common.Billing, common.LedgerStack, common.Kafka, common.KafkaIngest, common.Server, ...). Add new service dependencies here and to the Application struct.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.LedgerStack is the credits-aware ledger provider — do not construct ledger services outside it. Use common.WatermillNoPublisher (server publishes but does not subscribe), not common.Watermill.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire — DO NOT EDIT. Longest generated file (~600 lines); reference for dependency order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Credits-guarded ledger construction appears here — verify noop vs real implementation when debugging credits.enabled=false paths.

anti_patterns: Registering a namespace.Handler after initNamespace — the default namespace misses that handler's provisioning
Adding domain service calls in the startup sequence without error handling + os.Exit(1)
Adding an HTTP endpoint without adding its service to router.Config and the wire.go Application struct
Using common.Watermill instead of common.WatermillNoPublisher (server does not consume Kafka topics)

decisions.decision: cmd/server is the only binary registering namespace handlers
decisions.rationale: Namespace provisioning (ClickHouse table creation, Kafka topic creation, ledger account setup) must happen exactly once via the server startup path; other workers do not register these handlers.

decisions.decision: NotificationEventHandler runs as a goroutine in cmd/server's run.Group
decisions.rationale: It is lightweight and co-locating it with the API server simplifies deployment; the standalone notification-service binary remains available for horizontal scaling.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new domain service and wire it to an HTTP handler
